chunks.text: On July 20, 1969, humanity stood on the moon.
The mission of Apollo 11 was launched after many years of preparation.
Had it not been for Kennedy's speech of 1961
such a challenge to technology might never have begun.
The moment Armstrong set his foot upon the moon
people all over the world watched the scene.
His brief words became a gift to all humanity.
Without the dedication of countless engineer, that moment could not have happened.
Each person's contribution supported this single great feat.
On that day, humanity began to write new history beyond the earth.
This was a truly historic moment.
The moonlight shone upon the crew who had returned safely to the earth.
